title: So many headwinds for the ASX, but war is not one of them
description: Soft bank earnings growth and a lack of exposure to the booming artificial intelligence trade will keep a handbrake on the local sharemarket, fund managers warn.
content:
War is heating up again in the Middle East, sending the price of oil back towards $US80 a barrel, but it’s not the major concern for investors in Australia’s sharemarket as they face the prospect of another year of middling returns for the ASX.

title: TSMC posts record revenue in second quarter on AI demand
description: Revenue in the second quarter rose 36% from a year earlier to ​a record high on surging interest in artificial intelligence applications. On its ​last earnings call in April, the company predicted second-quarter revenue of between $39 billion and $40.2 billion. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing ​Co (TSMC) is a major supplier to companies including Nvidia and Apple.

title: Supreme Court's AI Rules Explained: Why India Wants Artificial Intelligence In Courts, But Not In Judicial Decisions
description: The Supreme Court is drafting rules to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in courts after AI-generated fake judgments surfaced in legal proceedings. Here's what the proposed framework means for judges, lawyers and litigants.

title: How rising AI costs are reshaping business decisions
description: From token limits and usage-based pricing to layoffs and AI sovereignty, companies are discovering that the economics of artificial intelligence matter as much as the technology itself.

title: TCS To Hire Up To 8,900 AI Engineers, Eyes Acquisitions In Cybersecurity
description: Tata Consultancy Services is building a team of up to 8,900 forward-deployed engineers and is also looking at AI-related acquisitions. The company believes artificial intelligence will create new business opportunities rather than hurt its outsourcing model.
